c4b6.summary: Use group-clustered JSON files instead of a flat zmem-style graph
c4b6.content:
## Status
accepted

## Context
zmem stored all nodes in a single graph.json which caused:
- Slow YAML processing (confirmed by user)
- Monolithic structure hard to extend
- No per-group backend flexibility

## Decision
Break memory into 4 default groups (project_dna, problem_tracking, decision_trail, code_history), each with its own JSON file and optionally its own backend provider.

## Consequences
+ Groups can independently use json/sqlite/vector backends
+ Clear semantic boundaries reduce cross-group noise
- Queries across groups require a fan-out step via MemStore

7dd1.summary: orjson as hard dependency, MtimeFileCache per JsonBackend
7dd1.content:
## Status
accepted

## Context
zmem used YAML which the user found notably slow in practice. orjson is 3-6x faster than stdlib json for typical payloads.

## Decision
orjson is a required dependency (not optional). All JSON reads go through MtimeFileCache (keyed by st_mtime float) to avoid redundant disk reads during scan/export within a single process lifetime.

## Consequences
+ Much faster repeated reads in scan/export flows
+ Cache invalidation is automatic on mtime change or explicit via invalidate()
- orjson requires a C extension; pure-Python fallback removed

1156.summary: validate_node_metadata uses dataclass field introspection to check Literal and basic types

992f.summary: zmem cli.py (587 lines), core/cache.py (MtimeFileCache), utils/ids.py ported as useful references

f0ee.summary: Node dataclass is frozen with private _tags/_metadata/_custom tuples exposed as public list/dict properties
